response.justification: The candidate has a broad software background spanning 15+ years and demonstrates strong testing culture and distributed systems exposure, but fails the most critical minimum qualification by a wide margin: C++ experience appears limited to roughly 6-7 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. Cloud compute platform depth is also surface-level — AWS CDK usage and Kubernetes deployments as an operator do not substitute for building large-scale compute infrastructure. The role demands a seasoned technical leader with deep C++ and cloud infrastructure ownership, neither of which this profile demonstrates.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship ethos with demonstrated TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development practices across multiple roles
High-traffic distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency techniques
Active knowledge-sharing through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects indicating leadership and mentoring aptitude
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent from the career narrative — ~6 months at National Instruments and a thesis project versus the required 8 years is a disqualifying gap
No evidence of owning or architecting cloud compute infrastructure at scale; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK for pricing logic and Kubernetes as a deployment target

No formal technical lead or engineering management track record; staff-level roles appear to be IC-focused without explicit team leadership, mentoring pipelines, or cross-team alignment responsibilities

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud compute platform depth
